sanskrit:
न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः।
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परं मम।।
transliteration: na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ | yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama ||
literal_meaning: That supreme abode of Mine is not illuminated by sun, moon or fire; having gone there one does not return.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes a trans-luminous state of consciousness beyond sensory perception where psychological time (birth-death cycle) ceases; it is the mind’s own self-luminous ground that no external source can reveal.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth: after witnessing the impermanence of every external support (career, relationships, health) the mind intuits an inner light that needs no reflection.
trigger_scenario: Lying in an MRI machine after a cancer diagnosis, lights off, you realize the radiance you’re searching for isn’t on the scan—it’s the awareness reading the scan.
anchor_text: Rest in the inner light untouched by sun, moon, or flame.
